refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained a phrase 'meet or exceed' which can be simplified to 'achieve' to keep it clear and atomic. The FR focuses solely on the required operational availability metric, avoiding any implementation details or how to achieve it, thus maintaining solution neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time system health monitoring and fault detection
Design modular redundant subsystems to enable failover
Establish predictive maintenance scheduling based on usage and condition data
Incorporate automated recovery and restart procedures after failure
Define operational policies and safety plans to minimize downtime
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design modular redundant subsystems to enable failover

coupling_check.notes: Selecting modular redundant subsystems supports independence by isolating failures and enabling failover without tightly coupling components. Care must be taken to avoid shared single points of failure.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that failover mechanisms may not cover all failure modes
Operational policies must align with technical redundancy to achieve availability targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs to complement redundancy and ensure comprehensive availability management.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for implementing modular redundant subsystems to enable failover, including coordination of redundancy and failover logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs system status and health data relevant to failover monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives and manages commands that may trigger failover or redundancy actions.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to failover events impacting safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication between redundant subsystems and external modules for failover coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the failover and redundancy logic is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Partial containment possible but failover logic would diffuse into other modules due to vehicle's broader responsibilities.
Failover logic is not core to teleoperation; would cause significant diffusion and coupling.
Safety Services relates to alerts but not full failover subsystem; moderate diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the DP of designing modular redundant subsystems for failover, as it centralizes redundancy logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may require clear internal sub-modularization to avoid internal diffusion.
Interfaces with Safety Services and Vehicle must be well-defined to prevent coupling leakage.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing modular redundant subsystems to enable failover directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined operational availability target by providing a logical solution to maintain system operation despite failures.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may require clear internal sub-modularization to avoid internal diffusion.
Interfaces with Safety Services and Vehicle must be well-defined to prevent coupling leakage.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and containment score (9) with minimal diffusion (2), making it the most appropriate IM boundary to realize the DP. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, indicating risk if chosen.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring system availability metrics (≥0.97) via the Telemetry interface and ensuring failover actions are correctly triggered and coordinated within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, safety alerts, and network communication supporting failover coordination.

recommended_revisions: Define clear internal sub-modules within NGHE Solution to manage complexity and avoid internal diffusion.
Establish strict interface contracts with Safety Services and Vehicle modules to prevent coupling leakage and maintain modularity.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mapping passes all gates with clear justification and feasibility, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks within the NGHE Solution module suggest the need for further decomposition. Defining clear internal sub-modules (sub-FRs) within the NGHE Solution will help manage complexity, reduce internal diffusion, and establish strict interface contracts, thereby enhancing modularity and safety assurance.

revise.why: To explicitly capture the internal functional requirements needed to manage complexity and modularity within the NGHE Solution, supporting the identified need for clear sub-modularization and interface management.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary for the DP.
High safety criticality requires strict modularity and interface control.
open_risks: Potential internal diffusion within NGHE Solution if sub-modules are not defined.
Coupling leakage risks with Safety Services and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Verification plans for sub-module functionality and interfaces.
Metrics demonstrating reduced diffusion and improved modularity after sub-module definition.
